creator: nikola.lecic@anthesphoria.net
text:
On Fri, Jan 13, 2012 at 03:26:52AM +0000
  in <201201130326.q0D3QqJW007471@freefall.freebsd.org>
  scheidell@FreeBSD.org wrote:

> I had two problems with this.
> #1, the patch to remove files/patch-GrkPolyComp.KMN didn't actually remove the file.

As far as I know, patches created with 'cvs diff -Nu' are not supposed
to delete files. They leave files empty and it's up to the committer
to remove them from the CVS (and for maintainers to note that in the
PR description). The examples of the same practice from my past PRs:

  http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/140239
  http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/136360
  http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/127895
  http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/118272

> #2, the patch for files/patch-GrkPolyComp103.KMN doesn't apply cleanly:
> 
> http://lorie.secnap.net/tb/index.php?action=display_markup_log&build=8-scheidell_AMD64&id=509#103
> 
> could you make sure you update your portstree, verity the patches and resubmit the patch?

My tree is up-to-date and the patch applies cleanly. There is some
lines in the patch that differ from the original only in trailing
spaces; maybe Gnats distorted some characters? Please try this file:

  http://anthesphoria.net/tmp/kmfl-sil-galatia-1.0.3.patch.gz

Thanks,
-- 
Nikola Lecic
